To: Marcons Bulk Chartering <bulk@marconsgroup.com>
Subject: MV RUBAIYAT HANIF / 58713 DWT / JINGTANG, CHINA / 27-29 NOV
 
DEAR CHARTERING DESK / MARCONS
GOOD DAY, HOPE ALL FINE AND WELL
 
PLSD TO HEAR FOR FIRM AND RATED BIZ FOR THE BELOW CANDIDATE 
 
MV RUBAIYAT HANIF
-- OPEN  JINGTANG, CHINA; 27-29 NOV
-- LAST CRGOES IRON ORE (CURRENT) - CLINKER , DRY DOCK  
-- DIRTIES OK
 
TRADING AREA: PG- JAPAN RANGE INCLUDING  SOUTH AFRICA , COMOROS ,
MOZAMBIQUE, MADAGASCAR ,MAURITIUS, KENYA, TANZANIA ( ALWAYS OUTSIDE PIRACY
ZONE) 
- NO GOA
 
VESSEL ' S DETAILS :
MV RUBAIYAT HANIF (EX- NEW VICTORY)
TYPE / BUILT: SDBC / 2008
YARD: TSUNEISHI HEAVY INDUSTRIES INC.
FLAG / CLASS / CALLSIGN / IMO: BANGLADESH / BV /S2BZ8 /9403047
DWT / DFT (SUMMER): 58,713 MT ON 12.828M SSW
TROPICAL DWAT 60,250 MTS / 13.095 M
LOA / BEAM / TPC: 189.99 M / 32.26M / 57.54 MT
GRT / NRT: 32,379/19,353
HOLDS / HATCHES /HATCH TYPE: 5 HO /5 HA / END FOLDING TYPE WEATHER-TIGHT
STEEL HATCH COVER
HATCH SIZES (LXB): NO1: 17.91X 17.31 M NO 2-4: 21.91 X 18.91 M NO5: 21.11 X
18.91 M
 
CARGO GEARS: 4 X 30MT FIXED TYPE SINGLE JIB DECK CRANE. 4 X 30 TON SWL (HOOK
OPERATION) OR 24 TON (GRAB OPERATION). RADIUS 26 M, OUTREACH FROM SHIPS RAIL
9.87 M UNDER NORMAL WORKING CONDITION.
 
GRABS: 12 CBM RADIO REMOTE CONTROL GVEN GRABS. THE VESSEL FITTED WITH 4
UNITS OF 12.0 M3 GRABS (MAX PERMITTED DENSITY 2.3 TON/M3). MAXIMUM CAPACITY
OF GRABS 15 MT.
 
FOR HEAVY CARGOES, MASTER SHALL HAVE THE RIGHT TO REMOVE SPILL PLATE OF GRAB
TO REDUCE VOLUME. GEARS/GRABS NOT TO BE USED UNLESS EXPRESSIVELY SPECIFIED
IN CP. (FOR GRAIN, FERTILIZER, SUGAR AND SIMILAR TYPE CARGO MINOR LEAKAGE
ACCEPTABLE).
 
GRAIN / BALE: 72,360.7 CBM /70,557.8 CBM
 
HOLD CAPACITY BREAKDOWN:
1: 12,361.2 / 11,900.5M3
2: 15,976.4 / 15,625.2M3
3: 14,510.6 /14,163.7M3
4: 15,971.4 /15,608.8 M3
5: 13,541.1 /13,259.6 M3
 
TANK TOP STRENGTH:
1: 17.0 MT/M2
2: 17.0 MT/M2
3: 18.0 MT/M2
4: 17.0 MT/M2
5: 17.0 MT/M2

SPEED AND CONSUMPTION AT SEA: BALLAST ABT 13.50 KTS AND LADEN ABT 12.00 KTS
ON ABT 28.00 MT VLSFO 380 CST ABT & 0.1 MT LSMGO FOR ME & AE.
 
ECO SPEED & CONSUMPTION AT SEA: BALLAST ABT 12.50 KTS AND LADEN ABT 11.5 KTS
ON ABT 22.00 MT VLSFO 380 CST & 0.1 MT LSMGO FOR ME & AE.
 
IN PORT WORKING: ABT 4.25 MT VLSFO 380 CST & ABT 0.2 MT LSMGO & IDLE ABT
2.50 MT VLSFO 380 CST & ABT 0.2 MT LSMGO.
 
CONSUMPTION MAY INCREASE BY ABT 1.0 MT VLSFO/DAY DURING
BALLASTING/DEBALLASTING/BALLAST EXCHANGE OPERATIONS AT PORT/SEA,OR IN COLD
WEATHER FOR BOILER USE.
 
- SPEED / CONS ARE ABT IN GOOD WEATHER UP TO MAX BF 4 AND SMOOTH SEA
CONDITIONS AND NO ADVERSE CURRENTS OR NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL, ON SSW
DRAFT, NOT IN CONGESTED AREAS OR PASSING THROUGH STRAITS OR AREAS WITH POOR
VISIBILITY AND PROVIDED NO BOTTOM FOULING DUE TO IDLE PERIODS OF 22 DAYS OR
MORE. AN ADMISSIBLE PERIOD OF GOOD WEATHER HAS TO BE A PERIOD OF  24
CONSECUTIVE HOURS RUNNING FROM NOON TO NOON. EXTRAPOLATION OF GOOD WEATHER
PERFORMANCE FOR BAD WEATHER PERIODS IS NOT ALLOWED. ADVERSE CURRENT HAS TO
BE TAKEN INTO ACCOUNT WHEN ASSESSING VESSEL'S SPEED AND CONSUMPTION.
HOWEVER, FAVORABLE CURRENTS ARE TO BE EXPRESSLY EXCLUDED FROM VESSEL'S
PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS. TOTAL TIME LOST AND OVER CONSUMED BUNKERS CALCULATIONS
SHALL BE BASED ON THE GOOD WEATHER PERIODS ONLY, MEANING THAT THE AVERAGE
GOOD WEATHER PERFORMANCE CANNOT THEN BE EXTENDED AND APPLIED OVER THE WHOLE
CHARTER DURATION INCLUDING BAD WEATHER. BAD WEATHER PERIODS TO BE
DISREGARDED WHEN CALCULATING THE TIME LOST AND THE BUNKERS OVER CONSUMED
THROUGH THE CHARTER PERIOD THAN THE WARRANTED TIME & CONSUMPTION, DURING
GOOD/BAD WEATHER PERIOD IF VSL UNDER CONSUME THAN THE WARRANTED MAXIMUM
CONSUMPTION AND OVERPERFORMED THAN THE MINIMUM WARRANTED SPEED IN THAT CASE
OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO CREDIT FOR TIME & BUNKER SAVED. IN CASES WHEN VOYAGES
ARE FROM SEA BUOY TO SEA BUOY ON VOYAGES LESS THAN 48 HOURS AND 24 HOURS
AFTER COSP/PRIOR EOSP OR COASTAL TRADE, ACTUAL CONSUMPTION FIGURES TO APPLY.
VESSEL HAS THE LIBERTY TO USE LSMGO FOR THE MAIN ENGINE WHEN SECA, ECA,
MANOEUVERING IN/OUT OF PORTS, CANALS, RIVERS, RESTRICTED AREAS, SHALLOW &
NARROW WATERS, FOR STARTING UP/TURNING ENGINES, BURNING SLUDGE AND IN
ADVERSE WEATHER, BAD/COLD WEATHER, HOLD CLEANING AND BILGE PUMPING. MASTER
HAS THE AUTHORITY TO DIVERT COURSE FOR SMOOTH RUNNING/SAFETY OF THE VESSEL.
SPEED AND CONSUMPTION FIGURES IN FAIR WEATHER WITHOUT ADVERSE CURRENT, POOR
VISIBILITY, CONGESTION, NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL UP TO BEAUFORT SCALE
FORCE 4 / DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 / WAVE AND SWELL COMBINED HEIGHTS UPTO 1.25 M.
ABOUT ON SPEED AND CONSUMPTION CLARIFIED AS MEANING 5% ALLOWANCE ON
CONSUMPTION AND 0.5 KNOTS ON SPEED, BOTH IN OWNERS FAVOR. IT IS UNDERSTOOD
THAT VESSEL NOT TO BE ORDERED TO SAIL ON ECONOMICAL SPEED/CONSUMPTION IN
PIRACY RISKS AREAS
ALL DETAILS ABOUT AND GIVEN IN GOOD FAITH ANY SAVING IN CONSUMPTION MUST BE
OFF-SET AGAINST ANY SPEED UNDERPERFORMANCE, ANY SAVING IN TIME MUST BE
OFF-SET AGAINST ANY EXCESS CONSUMPTION, ANY SAVING IN FO MUST BE OFF-SET
AGAINST INCREASED MDO AND VICE VERSA, AND ANY OVERALL SAVING ON INDIVIDUAL
PASSAGE(S) MUST BE SET OFF AGAINST AN OVERALL LOSS ON OTHER INDIVIDUAL
PASSAGE(S). OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO A CREDIT FOR ANY BUNKER SAVINGS, WHICH
ARE TO BE CALCULATED AS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN MAXIMUM QUANTITIES OF BUNKERS
ALLOWED TO CONSUME AS PER ABOVE AND THE QUANTITY OF BUNKERS ACTUALLY
CONSUMED DURING THE VOYAGE. THE PRICE OF THE BUNKER TO BE AS PER CP PRICE
AGREED DURING NEGO. OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO THE SAID CREDIT FOR BUNKER
SAVINGS IRRESPECTIVE OF WHETHER THERE IS ANY SPEED CLAIM. IN THE EVENT THAT
THERE IS A SPEED CLAIM, THE CREDIT FOR BUNKER SAVINGS SHALL BE SET-OFF
AGAINST ANY LOSS OF TIME TO REDUCE OR EXTINGUISH SPEED CLAIM, AND IN THE
EVENT BUNKERS SAVINGS ARE OF VALUE GREATER THAN SPEED CLAIM, OWNERS ARE
ENTITLED TO CREDIT FOR REMAINING BALANCE OF BUNKER SAVINGS AFTER SETTING OFF
SPEED CLAIM.OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO A CREDIT FOR ANY TIME SAVED, WHICH IS TO
BE CALCULATED AS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN MAXIMUM TIME THE SHIP WAS ALLOWED TO
COMPLETE THE VOYAGE AS PER ABOVE AND THE TIME ACTUALLY TAKEN TO COMPLETE THE
VOYAGE.OWNERS ARE ENTITLED TO THE SAID CREDIT FOR TIME SAVED IRRESPECTIVE OF
WHETHER THERE IS ANY OVER-CONSUMPTION CLAIM. IN THE EVENT THAT THERE IS AN
OVER-CONSUMPTION CLAIM, THE CREDIT FOR TIME SAVED SHALL BE SET-OFF AGAINST
ANY OVER-CONSUMPTION TO REDUCE OR EXTINGUISH OVER-CONSUMPTION CLAIM, AND IN
THE EVENT TIME SAVED IS OF VALUE GREATER THAN OVER-CONSUMPTION CLAIM, OWNERS
ARE ENTITLED TO CREDIT FOR REMAINING BALANCE OF TIME SAVED AFTER SETTING OFF
OVER- CONSUMPTION CLAIM.
 
ALL DETAILS ABOUT
